title: Parallel Web Systems hits $2B valuation five months after its last big raise
description: The AI agent-tool startup founded by former Twitter CEO Parag Agrawal has raised $100 million, led by Sequoia, months after raising a previous $100 million.

title: Bill Gurley, Jack Altman back startup Pursuit, which helps companies sell to government
description: On Wednesday, Pursuit announced a $22 million Series A round led by Mike Rosengarten, the co-founder of OpenGov, with big-name VCs participating.

title: Firestorm Labs raises $82M to take drone factories into the field
description: A defense startup just raised $82 million to put drone factories inside shipping containers and bring manufacturing to the front lines.

title: Founder of Shark Tank-backed startup Scholly sues his acquirer Sallie Mae | TechCrunch
description: Chris Gray is suing his startup’s acquirer, Sallie Mae, for wrongful termination and alleging it's selling student data through a subsidiary. Sallie Mae denies the allegations and vows to fight.

title: BCI startup Neurable looks to license its 'mind-reading' tech for consumer wearables | TechCrunch
description: The startup specializes in "non-invasive" "mind-reading" tech — a kind of neural data collection that, its CEO hopes, will have all sorts of consumer applications.

title: India's Snabbit closes $56M round as investor interest in on-demand home services heats up | TechCrunch
description: Snabbit now processes over 40,000 daily jobs and has cut costs sharply as it expands across cities and services.

title: Investors back Skye’s AI home screen app for iPhone ahead of launch | TechCrunch
description: Skye's new AI app attracted investors before it even launched — a sign of interest in a more AI-aware iPhone.

title: To buy this Bay Area home, you’ll need Anthropic equity | TechCrunch
description: Someone’s offering an unusual deal for a 13-acre property in Mill Valley, just north of San Francisco.

title: Why Tokyo is the most important tech destination of 2026 | TechCrunch
description: SusHi Tech Tokyo 2026 has four tightly defined technology domains, each backed by live demonstrations, dedicated exhibit floors, and sessions featuring the people actually building and funding these technologies globally.

title: India's Snabbit seeks fresh funding at a $400M valuation, sources say | TechCrunch
description: Snabbit has scaled rapidly, crossing 1 million jobs in March, amid growing investor interest.

title: Lachy Groom to back India startup Pronto at a $200M valuation, sources say | TechCrunch
description: This round, should it occur, would double the house-help startup's valuation in a matter of weeks.

title: Cohere acquires, merges with Germany-based startup to create a 'transatlantic AI powerhouse' | TechCrunch
description: Cohere, the Canada-based AI company that makes AI tools for businesses in regulated industries, announced Friday it would merge with Aleph Alpha, a German company that also builds AI systems for businesses and governments.

title: Two college kids raise a $5.1 million pre-seed to build an AI social network in iMessage | TechCrunch
description: Series, a social networking app that's grown popular on college campuses, announced a $5.1 million pre-seed round from some big names in tech.

title: Apple's new CEO, and why Elon Musk wants to buy Cursor for $60B | TechCrunch
description: Equity digs into what Apple's leadership transition means for startups and more of the week’s biggest deals, including SpaceX's $60B option on Cursor.

title: Uber CTO Praveen Neppalli Naga joins StrictlyVC SF | TechCrunch
description: StrictlyVC San Francisco adds Uber CTO Praveen Neppalli Naga to the stacked lineup. This event kicks off on April 30. Register now to save your seat.

title: Tim Cook is stepping down. What happens to Apple now? | TechCrunch
description: Watch as TechCrunch’s Equity podcast takes a closer look at Apple's new era, SpaceX's $60B option on Cursor, and more of the week's headlines.

title: Redwood Materials loses COO amid layoffs, restructuring | TechCrunch
description: Former Tesla executive Chris Lister is retiring, and at least three other VPs have left Redwood around the recent restructuring, TechCrunch has learned.

title: Bret Taylor's Sierra buys YC-backed AI startup Fragment | TechCrunch
description: Sierra, the AI customer service agent startup founded by technologist Bret Taylor, announced today that it has acquired the YC-backed French startup Fragment.

title: Meet Noscroll, an AI bot that does your doomscrolling for you | TechCrunch
description: Noscroll wants to cure doomscrolling with an AI bot that reads the internet for you.

title: Don't stop hiring humans — stop hiring the wrong humans, Artisan's founder says
description: Artisan may be known for their bold “Stop Hiring Humans” campaign but the reality is every founder needs to assemble the right team if they want to scale.

title: Era raises $11M to build a software platform for AI gadgets | TechCrunch
description: Era thinks that we will see many form factors of AI hardware, including glasses, rings, and pendants.

title: The first StrictlyVC of 2026 kicks off in a week in San Francisco | TechCrunch
description: StrictlyVC San Francisco is in just a week. Now’s the time to grab yourself a ticket. Join VCs and founders at Sentro Filipino on April 30.

title: Another customer of troubled startup Delve suffered a big security incident | TechCrunch
description: TechCrunch has confirmed that Delve was the compliance company that performed the security certifications for Context AI, the AI agent training startup that last week disclosed a security incident.
